---
title: "Small-Example"
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

In this example we show estimating the counterfactual mean $E[Y^d]$ under a
LMTP using TMLE via `TL_Task`.

```{r setup}
devtools::load_all(".")
library(TargetedLearning)


set.seed(123)
n <- 300

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate simple two-timepoint longitudinal data
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
W1 <- rnorm(n)
L1 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0.4 * W1)

A1 <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.5 * W1 - 0.7 * L1))

L2 <- rnorm(n, mean = 0.3 * W1 + 0.8 * A1 + 0.4 * L1)

A2 <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(-0.2 * W1 + 0.9 * L2))

Y <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(-0.3 + 0.4 * W1 + 0.6 * A1 + 0.9 * A2 + 0.5 * L2))

df <- data.frame(
  W1 = W1,
  L1 = L1,
  A1 = A1,
  L2 = L2,
  A2 = A2,
  Y = Y
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wrap data in LMTPData
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
ds <- LMTPData$new(
  data = df,
  A_cols = c("A1", "A2"),
  L_cols = list("L1", "L2"),
  W_cols = "W1",
  Y_col = "Y"
)

print(ds)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Define an LMTP: flip binary treatment at each time
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
mtp_flip <- mtp_discrete(
  map_fun = function(A, H) 1 - A,
  support = c(0, 1),
  name = "flip_binary"
)

policy_seq <- repeat_policy_over_time(mtp_flip, tau = ds$tau())

print(policy_seq)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Nuisance factory:
# - g_t via logistic regression
# - Q_t via logistic fluctuation / logistic regression recursion
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
nf <- LMTPNuisanceFactory$new(
  learners_g = stats::glm,
  policy_seq = policy_seq,
  A_type = "discrete",
  g_mode = "density",
  learners_g_extra_args = list(family = stats::binomial())
)

fluct <- LMTPFluctuationSubmodel$new(
  family = stats::binomial(),
  bounds = c(0, 1)
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build TL_Task and run
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
task <- make_lmtp_tl_task(
  ds = ds,
  policy_seq = policy_seq,
  nuisance_factory = nf,
  fluctuation = fluct,
  learners_Q = stats::glm,
  learners_Q_extra_args = list(family = stats::binomial())
)

fit <- task$run()

print(fit)

fit$estimate
fit$se
fit$ci

head(fit$eif)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Also estimate E[Y] under the identity policy
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
identity_seq <- identity_policy(
  A_type = "discrete",
  discrete_support = c(0, 1),
  tau = ds$tau()
)

nf_obs <- LMTPNuisanceFactory$new(
  learners_g = stats::glm,
  policy_seq = identity_seq,
  A_type = "discrete",
  g_mode = "density",
  learners_g_extra_args = list(family = stats::binomial())
)

task_obs <- make_lmtp_tl_task(
  ds = ds,
  policy_seq = identity_seq,
  nuisance_factory = nf_obs,
  fluctuation = fluct,
  learners_Q = stats::glm,
  learners_Q_extra_args = list(family = stats::binomial())
)

fit_obs <- task_obs$run()

print(fit_obs)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# Delta-method contrast: E[Y^d] - E[Y]
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
fit_diff <- lmtp_delta_contrast(
  fit1 = fit,
  fit0 = fit_obs,
  transform = "difference"
)

print(fit_diff)
fit_diff$estimate
fit_diff$ci
```
